name: Jerusalem Temple
shortDescription: The Jerusalem Temple was the central place of worship for the Israelites and later Jews, serving as the focal point for religious and national identity. It housed the Ark of the Covenant and was the site of sacrifices and festivals.
description: The Jerusalem Temple refers primarily to two successive temples located on the Temple Mount in Jerusalem. The First Temple, also known as Solomon's Temple, was built in the 10th century BCE and served as the primary sanctuary for the Ark of the Covenant and the center of Israelite worship. It was destroyed by the Babylonians in 586 BCE. The Second Temple was constructed upon the return from Babylonian exile in the late 6th century BCE, initially a modest structure, it was later significantly expanded and renovated by Herod the Great. This Second Temple stood for nearly six centuries and was a vibrant center of Jewish religious, political, and social life. It was ultimately destroyed by the Roman Empire in 70 CE during the First Jewish-Roman War. The Temple held immense significance, representing God's presence among the people and serving as the location for sacrifices, festivals, and communal prayer. While the physical Temple no longer stands, it remains a central symbol in Jewish tradition and eschatology, with prayers and hopes for its eventual rebuilding. In Jewish tradition, the Temple is also associated with various miraculous events and prophetic visions, further solidifying its sacred status. The Temple is viewed not only as a historical structure but also as a spiritual ideal and a symbol of divine connection.

location.cosmological: Located on the Temple Mount in Jerusalem, considered the navel of the world in some Jewish traditions, a point of connection between the earthly and divine realms.

characteristics: Housed the Ark of the Covenant (First Temple)

sources.title: The Temple in Time and Eternity
sources.author: Truman G. Madsen

sources.title: Daily Life in the Time of Jesus
